question_text: In the free-body diagram shown below, the only horizontal force on the block is the applied force. If the block accelerates to the right, which choice describes the net horizontal force?

answer_key.explanation: Weight and normal cancel vertically. With only one horizontal force, the net horizontal force is the applied force itself.
common_mistakes: Including weight in the horizontal balance.

visual.caption: Free-body diagram of a sliding block.
visual.altText: A block on a flat surface with three arrows showing the weight, the normal reaction, and a single horizontal applied force.
